solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def magicalString(self, n: int) -> int:
        if n <= 0:
            return 0
        s = [1]
        count = 1
        last = 1
        ones = 1
        while len(s) < n:
            if s[-1] == 1:
                s.append(2)
                last = 2
            else:
                s.append(1)
                last = 1
            if last == 1:
                ones += 1
        return ones
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a single loop to generate the magical string and count the number of 1's. It keeps track of the last character generated and alternates between 1 and 2. The time complexity is O(n) because it generates the string up to length n, and the space complexity is O(n) because it stores the magical string. The energy implications are low because it only uses a single loop and does not perform any complex operations.
